# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 162

This is turn 162. Turns 1 through 161 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 162.

## Turn 162 — nested sections close the remaining incidence bottlenecks

The frozen \(h_0\) submission is unchanged: 256 bytes, SHA-256  
`4f81b04ae00e737a244f2445d70b095333a427508958f11489e3cabdc0192fdc`. Its implicit tame relation, 2-core marking, and modulus-\(M\) exponent semantics remain as specified in the current [problem statement](sandbox:/mnt/data/problem_statement(5284).md).

### Nested active-intersection theorem

Retain an active block
\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad M=K/R,\qquad T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[C]}M,
\qquad C=Y/K.
\]

Let \(J\leq Y\) project onto \(C\), and put
\[
U=J\cap K,\qquad Z=U\cap R,\qquad W=UR/R\leq M.
\]

For every proper-image child occurring in the \(R=1\) or rank-one zero-edge recurrences:

1. \(W\) is a proper \(C\)-submodule of \(M\). Since \(T_0\) is the unique maximal submodule,
   \[
   W\leq T_0.
   \]

2. If \(K_W\) is the inverse image of \(W\) in \(K\), then
   \[
   U/Z\longrightarrow W
   \]
   is a section of the central extension
   \[
   1\longrightarrow R/Z\longrightarrow K_W/Z\longrightarrow W\longrightarrow1.
   \]

3. Once \(U\) has been obtained from this inner section, \(J\) is obtained from an outer section
   \[
   N_Y(U)/U\longrightarrow C.
   \]

Conversely, an admissible pair \((Z,W)\), an inner section, and an outer normalizer section reconstruct a unique embedded \(J\). Distinct embedded sections remain distinct incidence entries.

Thus broad subgroup-lattice enumeration is replaced uniformly by:

\[
(Z\leq R,\;W\leq T_0)
\quad+\quad\text{inner section}
\quad+\quad\text{outer section}.
\]

In particular:

- when \(R=1\), only \(C\)-submodules \(W\leq T_0\) are considered;
- when \(T_0=0\) and \(R\simeq C_2\), only \(U=1\) and \(U=R\) occur;
- in each scalar \(C_2\)-pushout of the \(|R|\geq4\) branch, an exact-image subgroup is either the whole cover or a complement.

This is the structural pattern behind Turn 161’s section enumeration, rather than a new casewise shortcut.

### Complete \(\lvert R\rvert\geq4\) expansion

I constructed
\[
Y_{320}=K_{64}\rtimes C_5,
\]
where \(K_{64}\) is a special exponent-four group with
\[
\Phi(K_{64})=Z(K_{64})=R\simeq C_2^2,\qquad K_{64}/R\simeq \mathbf F_2^4.
\]

The \(C_5\)-module is the additive group of \(\mathbf F_{16}\). The two central coordinates come from the invariant alternating forms
\[
b_a(v,w)=\operatorname{Tr}_{\mathbf F_{16}/\mathbf F_2}(avw^4),
\qquad a\in\mathbf F_4,
\]
with a \(C_5\)-invariant quadratic refinement. All three nonzero scalar pushouts have minus-type quadratic form.

For one fixed \(C_5\)-frame, the nested-section engine gives
\[
E(C_5)=1,\qquad E(C_{10})=7.
\]

For
\[
B=\mathbf F_2^4\rtimes C_5,
\]
there are exactly sixteen complements, hence
\[
E(B)=16^2E(C_5)-16E(C_5)=240.
\]

For each of the three scalar pushouts \(Y_\lambda\) of order \(160\):

- \(Y_\lambda\to B\) is nonsplit, so it has no complement;
- the proper-image intersection buckets \(U=1\) and \(U=R\) each contain sixteen embedded sections;
- the auxiliary split cover has \(S_0=E(C_{10})+E(C_5)=8\).

The rank-one zero-edge recurrence therefore gives
\[
\begin{aligned}
E(Y_\lambda)
&=4^3\bigl[-8+4(4+1)\bigr]
 -(16\cdot1+16\cdot7)\\
&=640.
\end{aligned}
\]

Finally,
\[
\begin{aligned}
E(Y_{320})
&=|R|^2(2-d_R)E(B)
  +\frac{|R|^2}{4}\sum_{\lambda\ne0}S_\lambda(B)\\
&=16(-2)(240)+4(3\cdot640)\\
&=0.
\end{aligned}
\]

Literal evaluation of the frozen \(h_0\) word independently returned \(240\) surjections per frame onto \(B\), \(640\) onto every \(Y_\lambda\), and \(0\) onto \(Y_{320}\). There were \(1024\) relation-compatible tuples per root frame, so the zero is an exact-image failure rather than absence of homomorphisms.

### Complete nonzero-edge certificate

I also constructed
\[
Y_{384}=(Q_8\times C_2)\rtimes S_4.
\]

Here
\[
R=Z(Q_8)\simeq C_2,\qquad
M=(Q_8\times C_2)/R,
\]
and \(M\) is the three-dimensional augmentation module of \(S_4\). Its radical and simple head are
\[
|T_0|=2,\qquad |V|=4.
\]

The \(S_4\)-action was lifted so that, for a radical lift \(t\),
\[
g(t)=t\,r^{\operatorname{sgn}(g)},\qquad r\in R.
\]
Consequently the radical edge is the nonzero sign class in
\[
H^1(S_4,T_0^\vee)\simeq\mathbf F_2;
\]
it is nonzero on exactly twelve of the twenty-four elements of \(S_4\).

Writing \(B=Y_{384}/R\), the complete child-first calculation is
\[
E(S_3)=1,
\]
\[
E(S_4)=16E(S_3)-4E(S_3)=12,
\]
and
\[
E(C_2\times S_4)
 =16E(C_2\times S_3)-4E(C_2\times S_3)
 =16\cdot6-4\cdot6
 =72.
\]

For \(B\to S_4\), the nested-section theorem finds exactly:

\[
16\text{ children with }U=1,\qquad
8\text{ children with }U=T_0.
\]

Hence
\[
E(B)=64\cdot12-16\cdot12-8\cdot72=0,
\]
and the genuine nonzero-edge recurrence gives
\[
E(Y_{384})=4E(B)=0.
\]

Literal frozen-word evaluation matched every node: \(1,6,12,72,0,0\) per fixed frame for
\[
S_3,\ C_2\times S_3,\ S_4,\ C_2\times S_4,\ B,\ Y_{384},
\]
respectively. The root has six tame frames. It still has \(5120\) relation-compatible tuples per frame, confirming that the nonzero-edge branch was exercised nontrivially.

**ESTABLISHED:** Proved the nested active-intersection theorem, replacing arbitrary subgroup enumeration by \(C\)-stable pairs \((Z,W)\) and two section problems. Used it to complete the full recursive expansion of an order-\(320\) \(|R|\geq4\) fixture and a genuine order-\(384\) nonzero-edge fixture. Every recurrence count matched independent literal evaluation of the frozen \(h_0\) word. No presentation change is indicated.

**GAP:** The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The generic compiler still needs canonical enumeration of the \(C\)-stable pairs \(Z\leq R,\ W\leq T_0\) and serialization of the resulting nested-section incidences for arbitrary large targets.

**NEXT:** Replace every remaining admissible-intersection search in the compiler by the nested-section theorem, using the module lattices of \(R\) and \(T_0\) as the only outer search. Then run the unchanged 256-byte submission and localize any rejection to its lowest framed certificate node before altering the word.
